title: Pungent Polish Sauerkraut Stew
description: A hearty and spicy Polish stew made with nuka-pickled cabbage, yuzu-kosho, and Polish sausage. Serve with crusty bread for a filling meal.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the Polish sausage until browned, then remove and set aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the nuka-pickled cabbage, caraway seeds, and yuzu-kosho. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cabbage starts to soften.
Add the chicken broth, flour (if using), and browned sausage back to the pot. Stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the cabbage is tender.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, with sour cream on the side (if using).
